content:
It’s time to believe in utopias again

10 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Made by the author in CanvaAn Invitation
I know things feel overwhelming right now. And there’s a good reason for that.
Life is overwhelming right now.
Our society is in the middle of a massive transition, and transition periods are dangerous. The uncertainty is palpable, especially for those of us who cannot afford to build and stockpile underground bunkers.
The main driver of uncertainty is of course generative artificial intelligence, but in reality, this development is part of a larger trend revealing the fragility and frustrations of our current system. Here are some key features of the dysfunction:
— Wages have not kept pace with inflation
— Most people do not like their jobs
— Corporations dominate politics
— Modern life requires excessive screen time
— Burnout is rampant
— Healthcare is a convoluted mess
— The majority of cities are designed for cars, not people
Feel free to add to the list. It is by no means comprehensive.
The point is, we have known for a while that things are not working when judged against the…
